notes: Lesson 3 Orientation (act 14, 'How markets find a price — the route', 972.96s, 7 long ~139s cues rescaled to the real MP3). Generic route-map orientation reusing econ.py SD idioms (market_axes/demand/supply/equilibrium/gap_marker/push_arrow): High-level map -> stall analogy -> tiny numeric example (Qd/Qs at $3/$5/$7) -> shortage/surplus mechanism -> two-equation solve (Q*=P*=5) -> misconceptions -> synthesis. Two iterations: round-1 authored+rendered (cue01 hit the >0.25s duration assert from 15fps rounding across ~11 play calls, trimmed a run_time so pace_to pads exact); round-2 fixed cue04's subtitle colliding with the headline band (moved UP3.0->UP2.55) and shortened cue02/03/05 bottom captions + shifted LEFT0.6 so they clear the shared 'Quantity' x-axis label (matching the short-caption pattern in act17). Per-cue checklist (formula legibility+correctness incl demand/supply lines & Q*=P*=5 & Qd/Qs 7.5/5/2.5, highlighted element matches narration, contrast, nothing off-frame/overlapping via theme.guard, chunk timing) all pass across all 21 frames. Final -ql 973.068s vs 972.96s audio (Δ0.108<0.5). ROUND-3 (final 1080p spot-check, this session): reading the 1080p spot frames caught cue0's two gloss sub-captions ('buyers: how much at each price' / 'sellers: how much at each price') rendering near-BLACK on the #0F1117 background (illegible) — root cause was color=rm.labels[i].color, where Manim's Text.color getter returns the group default black rather than the per-glyph INK_MUTED (the -ql 480p pass missed it). Fixed by coloring each gloss with its stop accent (C_DEMAND blue / C_SUPPLY amber / C_EQ green), which also ties it to the Indicated dot. Re-rendered ONLY cue0 at 1080p and re-muxed; re-read the final full-video frame @68s ('buyers' now legible blue) and chunk frame @82s ('sellers' legible amber). All other cues unchanged and still pass.

frames.notes: title/setup write-in, in-frame

Route map: 3 stops Demand(blue)/Supply(orange)/Equilibrium(green) drawn in sequence; per-stop gloss + 'next: draw the two curves and cross them'. Highlights each stop as narration names it; in-frame; legible.

Market-stall analogy: Buyers(blue,'want low prices') vs Sellers(orange,'want high prices') boxes, inward arrows, green 'price' node settling between; caption 'each side pulls; the price settles between them'. Clean, matches 'workshop metaphor' narration.
end-of-cue state, highlight+caption match narration, clears axis labels
title/setup write-in, in-frame

One good, three prices: Qd/Qs table $3->7.5/2.5, $5->5.0/5.0, $7->2.5/7.5 (economically correct), SD crossing, green equilibrium dot at $5; caption shortened to 'at $5 the two match — the price' + LEFT0.6 to clear Quantity axis label. Matches 'follow one concrete object'.
end-of-cue state, highlight+caption match narration, clears axis labels
title/setup write-in, in-frame

Mechanism: P=7 surplus (violet double-arrow between curves) + down-arrow (price falls) then shortage/push; green eq dot; caption 'gaps vanish only at the crossing' shortened+LEFT0.6 clears Quantity label. Correct: gaps close only at crossing.
end-of-cue state, highlight+caption match narration, clears axis labels
title/setup write-in, in-frame

Equations: demand(q)=9-0.8q, supply(q)=1+0.8q, 9-0.8q=1+0.8q, 1.6q=8 => q=5, Q*=5 P*=5 (all math correct). Subtitle 'Model it as two equations' moved to UP2.55 so it sits below the headline (was colliding at UP3.0). Legible MathTex, no overflow.
end-of-cue state, highlight+caption match narration, clears axis labels
title/setup write-in, in-frame

Separate nearby ideas: rose 'not the highest point' dot vs green crossing ('the crossing is the price'); then surplus gap arrow with caption 'a surplus is a gap, not a new curve' shortened+LEFT0.6 clears Quantity label. Matches misconception narration.
end-of-cue state, highlight+caption match narration, clears axis labels
title/setup write-in, in-frame

Synthesis: full route map all 3 stops colored, recap 'Demand slopes down · Supply slopes up' + 'Equilibrium = the crossing, where the gap is zero' + 'Now: three short parts + practice'. Clean, in-frame, matches 'return to the route'.
end-of-cue state, highlight+caption match narration, clears axis labels
